content:
```json
{
  "idea": "Multimodal Clinical Companion Foundation Model with Longitudinal EHR-Integrated Dialogue and Evidence Feedback",
  "idea_description": "A medical foundation model that continuously assimilates text, physical examination findings, imaging reports, and electronic health records across encounters, using evidence-based feedback loops to maintain coherent longitudinal patient understanding while aligning with clinical workflows and safety constraints. The model would support multimodal clinical interactions, answer diagnostic/prognostic queries, and recommend actionable next steps while preserving patient privacy and interoperability with existing healthcare systems.",
  "technical_plan": {
    "target_problem": "Real-world clinical AI gaps in multimodal consultation, longitudinal patient understanding, EHR integration, and safe clinical workflow alignment.",
    "core_method": "A multimodal transformer architecture trained on pre-visit consultations, physical signs, imaging reports, and longitudinal EHR records, augmented with evidence feedback from validated clinical guidelines, physician annotations, and audit logs.",
    "limitation_addressed": "Moves beyond AMIE-style text-based pre-visit consultations by adding imaging, physical signs, EHR integration, longitudinal coherence, and clinical workflow safety.",
    "expected_result": "A model that answers multimodal clinical questions, tracks patient progress, and supports safe, coherent care across encounters while retaining performance with existing healthcare infrastructure."
  },
  "lineage_connection": {
    "source_ideas": [
      "AMIE prospective study of text-based pre-visit consultations",
      "Medical Large Language Models for clinical question answering",
      "Transformer architectures for multimodal and longitudinal healthcare AI"
    ],
    "reused_parts": [
      "AMIE-style feasibility in pre-visit consultation text",
      "Clinical question answering using medical LLMs",
      "Transformer-style language modeling for medical text"
    ],
    "changed_parts": [
      "Add physical signs, imaging reports, and EHR records as multimodal inputs",
      "Introduce evidence feedback loops for guideline-based safety and coherence",
      "Train for longitudinal patient understanding across encounters"
    ],
    "why_next_step": "AMIE demonstrated feasibility for text-based consultations; this step builds toward continuous, multimodal clinical companionship by integrating beyond text with physical, imaging, and EHR data.",
    "lineage_risk": "If clinical guidelines and EHR feedback are insufficiently aligned with real-world workflow constraints, the model may fail to operate safely in existing healthcare systems."
  },
  "evaluation_plan": {
    "setup": "Evaluate on multimodal clinical consultation datasets, longitudinal EHR cohorts, and simulated clinical workflow tasks, including diagnostic reasoning, progress tracking, and safety-compliant recommendation generation.",
    "baselines": [
      "AMIE-style text-only pre-visit consultation models",
      "Single-modal medical LLMs trained only on text or imaging",
      "Rule-based clinical decision support systems"
    ],
    "metrics": [
      "Clinical question answering accuracy",
      "Longitudinal patient coherence score",
      "Clinical workflow alignment and safety compliance",
      "Model interpretability and evidence traceability"
    ],
    "risks": [
      "Multimodal training data may be sparse or inconsistent across modalities",
      "EHR integration may introduce privacy, bias, or interoperability barriers",
      "Evidence feedback loops may slow inference or reduce model efficiency"
    ]
  }
}
```
prompt:
You are a senior researcher in Clinical AI / Medical Large Language Models.
Based on your expertise, generate a new research idea for the following frontier question.

**Frontier Question**:
The AMIE prospective study demonstrates feasibility in text-based pre-visit consultations, but real-world clinical AI requires multimodal interaction (interpreting physical signs, reviewing imaging alongside conversation), longitudinal patient relationship management, and integration with electronic health records and clinical workflows. What architectural and training paradigm would enable a medical foundation model to serve as a continuous, multimodal clinical companion that maintains coherent longitudinal patient understanding across encounters while operating safely within existing healthcare systems?

Generate a new research idea for the frontier question.

Return ONLY valid JSON. Do not wrap it in Markdown fences.
{
  "idea": "<short name of the proposed idea, 5-15 words>",
  "idea_description": "<150-250 words describing the full research idea>",
  "technical_plan": {
    "target_problem": "<specific problem, dataset, population, or phenomenon>",
    "core_method": "<technical method or mechanism, not a vague goal>",
    "limitation_addressed": "<frontier limitation repaired by this idea>",
    "expected_result": "<scientific result or claim if successful>"
  },
  "lineage_connection": {
    "source_ideas": ["<paper title or source idea used by this proposal>", "..."],
    "reused_parts": ["<specific method, evidence, data, or problem framing reused from the sources>", "..."],
    "changed_parts": ["<specific modified, replaced, combined, or newly added part>", "..."],
    "why_next_step": "<why this idea follows from the provided context>",
    "lineage_risk": "<most likely way this source connection could be wrong>"
  },
  "evaluation_plan": {
    "setup": "<concrete experiment, benchmark, proof, or analysis setup>",
    "baselines": ["<baseline>", "..."],
    "metrics": ["<metric>", "..."],
    "risks": ["<risk>", "..."]
  }
}

metadata.system_prompt: You are a scientific lineage analyst and idea generator. Respond with a single JSON object inside ```json ... ``` fences matching the schema requested in the user prompt. No commentary outside the JSON.
